name: Upwords
description: Upwords is the three-dimensional Scrabble cousin invented by toy designer Elliot Rudell in 1982. Players spell words horizontally or vertically across a 10×10 board — but the twist is vertical: you can stack new letter tiles directly on top of existing ones (up to five high), turning CAT into COT into BOT into BOOT. Tall stacks score more, so the board literally grows upward as the game goes on. Quick to learn, devious in late-game play.
